meta.description: The first public-safe benchmark pack for Role Foundry's autoresearch loop. Every episode is student-visible and grounded in public curriculum only.
meta.honesty_note: This pack is valid for public training, iteration, and regression checks. It is not a sealed certification pack. Teacher-only families are explicitly blocked pending rewrite.

recommended_loop: pick one episode and keep the mutation surface narrow
run only public/student-visible work for that episode
record changed files plus a short keep/discard note
run the recommended verifier commands before scoring the result

episodes.title: Name the apprentice in the opening screenful
episodes.student_prompt: Rewrite the first screenful so a new judge immediately sees who the student is, who the teachers are, and that the apprentice is building Role Foundry itself.
constraints: Keep demo mode honest
Do not introduce auth, Privy, or fake live wiring
Leave behind a concise receipt trail
public_checks: Frontend Apprentice is named explicitly
Robin + Neo are named explicitly as teachers
The story says the apprentice is building Role Foundry itself

episodes.title: Tighten the README intro around the dogfood loop
episodes.student_prompt: Refactor the README intro so it frames the repo as a teacher → student → evaluation loop, not a generic agent platform.
constraints: Keep the repo description concrete and judge-facing
Avoid fake future-looking claims
Preserve quickstart clarity
public_checks: README explains the named apprentice loop
README does not read like a generic marketplace or infra pitch
Demo-mode honesty remains explicit

episodes.title: Make student-visible versus teacher-only surfaces explicit
episodes.student_prompt: Clarify the scenarios page so a judge can tell what the student sees, what the teacher sees, and why the boundary matters.
constraints: Do not add teacher-only prompt text
Explain the contract boundary in plain language
Keep the slice scoped to one page or one doc
public_checks: Student-visible and teacher-only surfaces are labeled clearly
Prompt-pack copy references public curriculum only
No teacher-only rubric leaks into student-visible text

episodes.title: Explain the prompt-pack contract on the run detail page
episodes.student_prompt: Improve the run detail page or docs so it is obvious that the student prompt pack contains public curriculum only, while teacher-only evaluation remains separate.
constraints: No hidden prompt text
No core contract churn in runner_bridge
Leave a short receipt note
public_checks: Student prompt pack is described as public-only
Teacher-only evaluation is described without leaking prompts
The explanation stays concrete, not hand-wavy

episodes.title: Show why Run 2 is better with concrete deltas
episodes.student_prompt: Add or refine a surface that compares two runs with explicit score movement instead of vague improvement language.
constraints: Use concrete counts, rates, or receipts
Keep demo-vs-live honesty intact
Do not invent unimplemented telemetry
public_checks: At least two runs are compared
Improvement is visible as a delta
No fake instrumentation is implied

episodes.title: Explain why deltas matter more than vibes
episodes.student_prompt: Write or tighten one explanatory paragraph that tells judges why visible deltas matter more than a generic better/worse claim.
constraints: Stay short and legible
Do not leak teacher-only prompts
Keep the explanation grounded in this repo
public_checks: The copy argues for concrete deltas
The copy stays specific to Role Foundry
The copy does not oversell live capability

episodes.title: Make the proof bundle legible at a glance
episodes.student_prompt: Improve one surface so a judge can quickly inspect the receipt summary, changed files, transcript evidence, and policy snapshot for a strong run.
constraints: Keep the surface judge-friendly
Do not claim live artifact backends that do not exist
Leave a receipt note
public_checks: Proof bundle contains changed files, transcript evidence, and policy snapshot
Receipts are easy to scan
No fake storage or API wiring is claimed

episodes.title: Strengthen transcript evidence without widening scope
episodes.student_prompt: Tighten the proof-bundle explanation so transcript evidence feels useful and auditable, not decorative.
constraints: Keep the scope to one explanatory slice
No new live integrations
Preserve demo-mode honesty
public_checks: Transcript evidence is explained as audit material
The slice stays narrow
The copy does not imply full production artifact plumbing

episodes.title: Refuse fake live wiring in judge-facing copy
episodes.student_prompt: Audit one judge-facing surface and remove any language that could be read as claiming live auth, live control-plane state, or finished partner-track wiring.
constraints: Be explicit about what is still stubbed
Do not add compensating fluff
Keep the change narrowly scoped
public_checks: Demo-only versus live-mode status is explicit
No fake auth or control-plane claims remain in the edited slice
The result is clearer, not emptier

episodes.title: Keep the slice narrow and skip partner-track theater
episodes.student_prompt: Respond to a polished but vague improvement request by shipping one coherent slice only. Do not add partner-track stickers, decorative integrations, or broad repo churn.
constraints: No partner work
No runner_bridge core-contract churn unless absolutely necessary
Leave behind a short keep-discard note
public_checks: The response stays within one coherent slice
No decorative partner-track language appears
Core contracts remain stable unless the task truly requires otherwise

episodes.title: Promote one failed theme into public curriculum safely
episodes.student_prompt: Take one failure theme and turn it into a public curriculum lesson without quoting or reconstructing teacher-only prompt text.
constraints: Use sanitized lesson wording only
Do not reveal grading internals
Leave a policy snapshot
public_checks: The new lesson reads as a public theme
Teacher-only wording is not quoted or reconstructed
The result is useful for the next public training loop

episodes.title: Refresh the student prompt pack from public themes only
episodes.student_prompt: Update one student-facing prompt-pack surface so it references public curriculum plus promoted public failure themes only. Keep teacher-only evaluation separate.
constraints: No teacher-only prompt text
No grading-rubric leakage
Keep the change focused to one surface
public_checks: Student prompt pack references public curriculum only
Teacher-only evaluation remains separate
Public failure themes read like lessons, not leaked exam text

episodes.title: Use user-visible locators for a curriculum-page regression check
episodes.student_prompt: Write or improve a regression check for the scenarios/curriculum page using Playwright best-practice locator strategies: getByRole, getByText, or getByLabel instead of brittle CSS selectors. The check should verify that public curriculum cards render and holdout cards are visually distinct.
constraints: Target an existing Role Foundry screen (app/scenarios.html)
Use user-visible locators only — no data-testid or CSS-path selectors
Do not claim a full Playwright CI harness ships if it does not
Leave a regression-check receipt
public_checks: Locators use role, text, or label — not CSS selectors or test IDs
Check targets the real curriculum page, not a hypothetical surface
Check distinguishes public curriculum cards from holdout cards
No overclaim about Playwright CI pipeline if one does not exist

episodes.title: Isolate a run-detail regression check from shared state
episodes.student_prompt: Write or improve a regression check for the run detail page that follows Playwright test-isolation best practices: the check should not depend on state left by other checks, should set up its own data context, and should verify proof-bundle rendering independently.
constraints: Target an existing Role Foundry screen (app/run.html)
Each check must be independent — no shared mutable state between checks
Keep the scope to one check or one small check file
Do not invent runtime artifact backends that do not exist
public_checks: Check sets up its own data context instead of relying on prior test state
Check targets the real run detail page, not a hypothetical surface
Check verifies proof-bundle rendering (changed files, policy, transcript)
No overclaim about end-to-end infrastructure if it is not wired

episodes.title: Write a focused review comment on a narrow RF diff
episodes.student_prompt: Given a small diff that changes copy on app/scenarios.html, write a structured review comment that is specific to the lines changed, actionable (says what to do, not just what is wrong), and separates must-fix issues from optional style suggestions.
constraints: Target an existing Role Foundry surface (app/scenarios.html)
Review comments must reference specific lines or hunks, not the file in general
Do not leak teacher-only prompt text or rubric content in the review
Keep feedback proportionate — a one-line copy fix does not need an architecture lecture
public_checks: Review comment references specific lines changed, not the whole file
Comment distinguishes must-fix from nice-to-have
Comment is actionable — says what to change, not just what is wrong
No teacher-only content leaks into the review

episodes.title: Separate blocking concerns from nits in an RF change review
episodes.student_prompt: Given a multi-hunk diff that touches app/run.html and app/data.js, triage the review into blocking issues (correctness, honesty, contract violations) versus nits (style, naming, whitespace). Blocking issues get explicit fix requests; nits get a single grouped suggestion. Keep the review proportionate to the change's risk.
constraints: Target existing Role Foundry surfaces (app/run.html, app/data.js)
Blocking issues must cite the specific hunk and explain the risk
Nits must be grouped, not scattered as individual comments
Do not inflate minor style issues into blocking concerns
Do not invent problems that are not present in the diff
public_checks: Blocking issues and nits are clearly separated
Blocking issues cite specific hunks and explain risk
Nits are grouped rather than scattered
Review is proportionate — no inflation of minor issues

episodes.title: Prevent disclosure-panel blips with Alpine show/cloak patterns
episodes.student_prompt: Improve one disclosure-heavy Role Foundry slice using Alpine.js show/cloak patterns so hidden-by-default content does not flash before Alpine initializes and reveal/hide behavior stays tied to explicit Alpine state instead of ad-hoc DOM toggles.
constraints: Target an existing Role Foundry screen (app/scenarios.html or another real RF surface)
Use Alpine-visible state + directives only — no imperative DOM query-selector choreography
If content starts hidden, prevent pre-init blips with x-cloak or template-based rendering
Leave a UI-state receipt
public_checks: Disclosure behavior is driven by Alpine state, not manual DOM mutation
Hidden-by-default content does not blip onto the screen before Alpine initializes
Change stays scoped to a real teacher-source or curriculum UI surface
No copied Alpine doc prose or GitHub thread text appears

episodes.title: Use Alpine bindings to keep active evidence state legible
episodes.student_prompt: Improve one existing run-detail or score surface so the active filter, evidence tab, or disclosure state is driven by Alpine class/attribute binding instead of styling drift. The selected state should be obvious to judges and stay in sync with the underlying Alpine state.
constraints: Target an existing Role Foundry screen (app/run.html, app/scorecard.html, or another real RF view)
Use Alpine x-bind / :class / :aria-* style binding rather than manual class toggles
Keep the slice narrow and judge-facing
Do not invent live backends or broaden product scope
public_checks: Active or selected state is visibly driven by Alpine bindings
UI semantics or classes stay in sync with the underlying state
Change targets a real RF evidence or score surface
No overclaim about live runtime or artifact plumbing is introduced

promotion_readiness.summary: Public Benchmark Pack v1 is ready to promote as the repo's public-safe benchmark pack for public training and regression loops. It is not a sealed certification pack.

B002.label: Rubric completeness
B002.expectation: Every public episode maps to a complete public rubric template.

B003.label: Weight normalization
B003.expectation: Each public rubric template sums to 1.0.

B005.label: Provenance coverage
B005.expectation: Every public episode cites its public source scenario and contract docs/specs.

B006.label: Promotion readiness clarity
B006.scope: Ready for public pack promotion with named limits only.
